subject: DCAE R6 updates
commit_message:
DCAE R6 updates

Helm chart updates to pull in latest version of components:
  bootstrap loads blueprints into DCAE inventory
  bootstrap configures k8splugin for JKS CA store
  inventory uses generated CA cert
  cloudify manager loads type file to synch clamp and dcae
    policy plugins
  dashboard skips bad blueprints from inventory
  bootstrap loads latest pgaas and sshkeyshare plugins
  bootstrap runs as non-root user

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2049
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-1938
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2097
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2120
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2072
Signed-off-by: Jack Lucas <jflucas@research.att.com>
Change-Id: I12f37ccc85ebfefba10e7ec1113da6474927ba57

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 2:

This time, DCAE came up fully.

Here is my conjecture on what happened during the previous deployment:

The ves collector (like several other DCAE components that are responsible for data collection or analysis) are deployed by Cloudify Manager, not (directly) by Helm, using a plugin that we wrote to deploy into k8s.  The plugin creates a k8s deployment (also often a k8s service as well) and then waits for the container(s) to become ready.  After a configurable interval (default, 30 minutes), if the containers are not ready, the plugin declares a failure and deletes all of the k8s entities (deployment, service, etc) that it created.

I suspect that the plugin actually created the k8s deployment and service for the VES collector but timed out waiting for the VES collector to become ready, and then deleted the deployment and service. I am not sure why the VES collector never became ready, and there's no way to tell without logs.  (And there is no way to be absolutely certain my conjecture is correct.)

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

Nevertheless, this changes doesn't seem to put OOM in a worse situation.
